extraction.one_line_summary: BALLERINI David won the Krakow sprint from the blue-and-white position after CAVENDISH unclipped with 1km to go, while EVENEPOEL Remco sealed GC with a 50km solo attack on Stage 4
tactical_patterns: EVENEPOEL's 50km Stage 4 attack created 1:52 GC gap, revealing willingness to gamble massively early in week-long races.
Trek-Segafredo worked hardest to close break despite ACKERMANN riding for Bora-Hansgrohe, suggesting internal sprint hierarchy confusion.
BALLERINI launched from blue-white position after STUYVEN opened sprint as classics rider, exploiting non-pure-sprinter leadout fatigue.

rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: Four stage race starts yielding four GC wins including first World Tour stage race shows abnormal conversion rate
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: negative: Mechanical foot-pedal issue at 1km negates positioning work, suggests equipment reliability concerns ending comeback attempt
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: neutral: Led out to perfection but beaten by BALLERINI despite Giro points jersey form, revealing possible fatigue
rider_signals.STUYVEN Jasper: negative: Classics specialist opening sprint for Trek indicates ACKERMANN leadout void and team desperation for stage win
rider_signals.BALLERINI David: positive: First World Tour win via late positional patience shows development beyond Deceuninck support role
